description: Snapp (also called NPG or mts-nature) is Springer Nature's proprietary submission system for Nature-branded journals. Separate from ScholarOne used by other Springer journals.
step_by_step: 1. Create a nature.com account (distinct from ScholarOne)
5. Enter title, abstract, and author information
6. Paste COVER LETTER in text box — do NOT upload as file
7. Upload main manuscript (Word file preferred; figures embedded AND uploaded separately)
8. Upload each figure as separate high-res file (PDF or TIFF ≥ 600 dpi)
9. Complete the Reporting Summary (mandatory for life sciences papers)
10. Upload Extended Data separately (counts toward figure limit)
11. Upload Supplementary Information as single file if possible
12. Declare all competing interests (all authors, including 'none')
13. Add Data Availability and Code Availability statements
common_errors: No Reporting Summary → system flags as incomplete for life sciences submissions
Missing Data Availability statement → required for all Nature journals
Cover letter uploaded as file → editors prefer text box entry
Extended Data not separated from main figures → exceeds figure limit
Competing interests missing for any co-author → submission flagged

file_requirements.main_document: Word (.docx) with figures embedded (also upload separately)
file_requirements.figures: PDF or TIFF ≥ 600 dpi; named Figure 1, Figure 2 ...
file_requirements.reporting_summary: Mandatory — download from Nature website for your study type
file_requirements.extended_data: Separate from main figures; same format requirements
file_requirements.cover_letter: Paste in text box — NOT uploaded as file
